avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: No significant change is expected. The snowpack will be firm, icy and generally stable all in locations  Coverage is good on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres and is extensive on the White Mounth. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A dry and bright period is expected with the freezing level dropping to 450 metres overnight, then rising to 750 metres during the day. Winds will be Northerly light to moderate through Friday night, backing South during the daytime while steadily increasing to become fresh.
Observed Weather Influences: A predominantly dry day with the freezing level around 900 metres. Moderate to light North-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is generally well consolidated and has good stability throughout. The snow surface is firm, softening slightly during the day below 800 metres and where exposed to direct sun. Despite the recent thaw, coverage remains good on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will be well bonded, very firm and stable. Any new snow deposits are not expected to be significant. The snow cover is patchy in places however significant areas will remain in some corries, gullies and around some summits. These areas will be very firm and mostly exist on North through East to South-East aspects above around 750 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A ridge of high pressure will bring mostly dry, cold conditions with moderate South-Westerly winds. A few light showers possible overnight falling as snow above around 500 metres. The freezing level will be around 500 metres rising slightly thorugh the day.
Observed Weather Influences: A mainly dry day with moderate North-Westerly winds. The freezing level fell to around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable. Snow cover is patchy in places with some significant areas remaining in corries, gullies and around summits above around 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The diminished snowpack will continue to consolidate  and refreeze on all aspects and levels and will be stable. Greatest amounts are mostly above 950m on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Patchy and snow ribbons below.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Cold and clear conditions expected over the forecast period with summit  temperatures of minus 5 deg C expected overnight, a few degrees warmer during the afternoon. Winds generally light South-Westerly. A dry bright day is expected.
Observed Weather Influences: A cooler day with occasional showers falling as light sleet and snow at summit levels for a time, generally clearing by midday. Winds becoming light during the afternoon. Freezing level around 1000m.
ObservedSnowStability: The diminished snowpack has consolidated in the cooler conditions, a slight surface crust has developed but the snowpack, although soft and moist to depth at all altitudes, is generally stable throughout.  Greatest amounts lie above 950 metres on North-West through North to Easterly aspects. Ribbons and patches of snow below this level but above 800m.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Colder conditions will maintain a consolidated snowpack. During the morning the surface layer will be frozen, firm and icy on all aspects some softening may take place after midday. Deepest snow amounts will exist on North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres other aspects will have lesser amounts. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will be around 700 metres, it is expected to be dry and clear, maybe even sunny. Winds will be light Southerly at first, strengthening through the day becoming moderate or strong.
Observed Weather Influences: Colder conditions last night affected the area with some light new snow falling mainly above 850 metres. It was a dry morning with just the odd light isolated shower in the afternoon. The freezing level fell to around 900 metres, winds were light to moderate from a Westerly direction.
ObservedSnowStability: The overnight cooler conditions have stabilised the moist snowpack of recent days. The dusting of new snow that arrived is not deep or extensive and exists mainly above 850 metres. The snowpack is generally stable on all aspects and is often firm and icy above 900 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The older snowpack will remain firm and stable. Isolated and shallow areas of windslab will be present, mainly on North to East aspects above 1100 metres with features such as gully tops and corrie rims most affected. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will remain dry with moderate, variable winds which increase from the South in the afternoon. The freezing level will be below the snowline.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a mainly dry day with fresh West-South-Westerly winds easing and veering in the afternoon. The freezing level was around 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The older snowpack is generally stable, with a firm surface above 900 metres. Isolated and shallow areas of moderately bonded windslab are present, mainly on North to East aspects above 1100 metres with features such as gully tops and corrie rims most affected. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to consolidate and become firm well bonded and stable throughout. The greatest extent of snow will be above 800 metres in areas such as coire back walls, flanks, and burn lines. Large cornices will continue to overhang North-West to Northerly aspects.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Light Westerly winds will increase to moderate through the afternoon and back to become Southerly in direction.  Apart from a trace of new snow in the early evening, it is expected to be dry throughout with clear skies. The 600 metre freezing level will rise to 800 metres during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Mainly dry with occasional light snow showers on moderate Westerly winds. The freezing level steadily lowered to around 1000 metres by midday.
ObservedSnowStability: There is a dusting of new snow on all aspects above 900 metres. The old snowpack continues to consolidate and has developed a firm crust above 950 metres, stability is generally improving. Large unstable cornices overhang North-West to Northerly aspects. The avalanche hazard is Low.
